text: Significant token movements and staking activity are occurring within the Hyperliquid ecosystem. A whale known as TechnoRevenant unstaked approximately $90 million worth of HYPE tokens, raising speculation about potential market impact. Meanwhile, liquidity pools on UltraSolid offer high APRs for HYPE pairs, attracting yield farmers. The protocol has also burned over 43 million HYPE tokens through spot fees, gas fees, and protocol fees, supporting tokenomics and buyback mechanisms.
author: elfa_2039744135381516646

text: The Hyperliquid ecosystem is expanding with new product launches and partnerships. Byreal, a Solana-based DEX powered by HyperliquidX liquidity, launched AI agent skills enabling over 1,000 autonomous agents to trade and manage liquidity pools. Byreal also introduced perpetuals with up to 40x leverage on crypto and real-world assets, plus new liquidity provider tools like AutoSwap, Compound, and Rebalance. Additionally, HyperliquidX added spot trading for $RIVER token and listed HYPE/EUR on Bybit EU with fixed earn options. These developments demonstrate growing adoption and utility of Hyperliquid's infrastructure.
author: elfa_2039704859700515128

text: HIP-3, a Hyperliquid Improvement Proposal, has gained significant traction, now accounting for 30% of Hyperliquid's total open interest. This upgrade enables permissionless market creation and supports real-world asset perpetual contracts, including commodities like oil and equities. Pyth oracle integration powers HIP-3 markets, providing 24/7 pricing and cross-asset capabilities, further solidifying Hyperliquid's market leadership in perpetual DEX fees and volume.

text: A major security incident occurred on Solana's largest perpetual DEX, Drift, where an attacker compromised an admin key and manipulated oracles to drain $285 million. This event highlights ongoing risks in DeFi, especially around multisig security and oracle robustness. In contrast, Hyperliquid's design avoids single admin keys and uses stake-weighted validator consensus, which has so far prevented similar exploits. The incident has sparked community discussions on improving protocol security and multisig timelocks.
author: elfa_2039833628218408987

text: Hyperliquid (HYPE) continues to strengthen its position as a leading perpetual DEX with growing market share and innovative features. The platform's HIP-3 upgrade enables permissionless market creation and supports real-world asset perps like oil and silver, driving significant trading volume and sustainable revenue. Hyperliquid's architecture avoids single admin key risks, enhancing security compared to recent exploits on other platforms. The protocol also features a 3/4 multisig securing $4 billion in assets, with no timelock but strong community confidence. Additionally, Hyperliquid's USDC supply hit a new all-time high of $6.04 billion, indicating strong demand and deep liquidity. The platform is actively integrating AI trading assistants and hosting agent-based trading competitions, further expanding its ecosystem.

text: Ledger CTO: Drift Attack Method Identical to Bybit Hacking; Attackers Possibly North Korean Hackers Ledger CTO Charles Guillemet stated that the Drift Protocol hack was not caused by a smart contract vulnerability, but rather by a breach of the protocol's multisig mechanism. The hackers appear to have gained control of the multisig holder's device or stolen enough private keys through long-term infiltration, misleading operators into approving malicious transactions. This attack method is highly similar to the attack on Bybit last year, which was suspected to be linked to the North Korean hacking group (DPRK). A large amount of the lost funds have already been transferred to wallet addresses controlled by the hackers. Guillemet called on the industry to improve endpoint detection capabilities and adopt hardware-supported plaintext signatures to prevent such operational-level security risks. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Drift's Investigation On the Exploit So Far > This was not the result of a bug in Drift's programs or smart contracts > There is no evidence of compromised seed phrases > The attacker pre-position access using durable nonce accounts > Attacker obtained sufficient multisig approvals (2/5 multisig approval) > Execute a malicious admin transfer within minutes, gaining control of protocol-level permissions > Use that control to introduce a malicious asset and remove all pre-set withdrawal limits > Drift confirms approximately $280M was withdrawn from the protocol. > All deposits into borrow/lend, vault deposits and funds deposited for trading are affected. Source:

text: CryptoQuant data shows that as of the end of March, apparent demand for Bitcoin (a measure of change in demand relative to new production) was negative by approximately 63,000 coins, meaning that new demand was insufficient to offset selling by existing holders.The report states that retail and other market participants sold more than institutional investors bought; whale addresses have shifted from continuous accumulation to net selling, and selling has accelerated since the fourth quarter of 2025. Demand from US investors also weakened, and the Coinbase premium turned negative again. — link
